title: What is tokenization, and why does it affect cost and quality?
prompt:
What is tokenization, and why does it affect cost and quality?

*Answer in the editor as you would out loud in an interview (a few sentences to a short paragraph is fine). Then use 💡 Hint, 🧠 AI feedback, or 📗 Show answer.*
reference_solution:
Tokenization splits text into sub-word units that the model processes. APIs charge per token, so tokenization directly determines cost. Common English words map to single tokens, but rare terms, technical jargon, non-English text, and numbers get split into many sub-tokens — inflating cost significantly. A 500-word English prompt might be 650 tokens, but the same content in Japanese could be 1,200+.

**Takeaway:** Tokenization shapes cost, quality, and reasoning. Test your domain vocabulary against the tokenizer early.
hint_notes: Tokenization shapes cost, quality, and reasoning. Test your domain vocabulary against the tokenizer early.
